metrics.context_qualifier: Emergency feeding program for stray animals during COVID-19 lockdown in Maharashtra, covering a three-month peak period.

counterfactual_baseline.description: Animals would have been left starving on the road due to the pandemic lockdown.

metrics.evidence_quote: we scaled up our feeding program to cover over 5 Lakh meals for stray animals who were left starving on the road due to the pandemic.

metrics.context_qualifier: Animals medically rehabilitated at YODA's shelter in 2020, including dogs, cats, farm, wild, birds, and other small animals.

counterfactual_baseline.description: Many homeless animals are injured or unwell and left to die due to lack of medical facilities in India.

metrics.evidence_quote: 18,254 animals were medically rehabilitated at our shelter in 2020.

metrics.context_qualifier: Approx. 17,800 animals successfully rehomed or rehabilitated and released from YODA's shelter in 2020.

counterfactual_baseline.description: Not reported explicitly, but implies these animals would not have been rehomed or successfully rehabilitated without intervention.

metrics.evidence_quote: Out of those, we have had approx. 17,800 success stories of animals being rehomed or rehabilitated and release.

metrics.context_qualifier: Homeless animals vaccinated in 2020, primarily dogs and cats, to reduce rabies and stray population.

counterfactual_baseline.description: India has over 30 million stray dogs, with over 20,000 people dying of rabies annually. Animals would remain at risk.

metrics.evidence_quote: In 2020, we have vaccinated 8000 animals and Sterilized 1,822 homeless animals

metrics.context_qualifier: Animals successfully adopted through YODA's program in 2020, primarily dogs and cats.

counterfactual_baseline.description: Not reported explicitly, but implies these animals would not have found homes without YODA's adoption program.

metrics.evidence_quote: In 2020, we have successfully homed 2489 animals

metrics.context_qualifier: Homeless animals sterilized in 2020, primarily dogs and cats, to control stray population and reduce disease.

counterfactual_baseline.description: India has over 30 million stray dogs, with over 20,000 people dying of rabies annually. Stray population would continue to grow.

metrics.evidence_quote: In 2020, we have vaccinated 8000 animals and Sterilized 1,822 homeless animals

metrics.context_qualifier: Retired police dogs from explosive and drug detection teams successfully rehabilitated and rehomed through YODA's program.

counterfactual_baseline.description: Many animals with behavioral issues, including retired service dogs, end up being put down internationally.

metrics.evidence_quote: 50 Retired Police dogs from explosive and drug detection teams were successfully rehabilitated and rehomed
metrics.metric_name: Retired Police Dogs Rehabilitated and Rehomed

significant_events.source_quote: We organized a robust emergency daily feeding program for thousands of street animals around Maharashtra who were left to fend for themselves during public lockdown periods.
significant_events.summary: Organized a robust emergency daily feeding program for thousands of street animals across Maharashtra during public lockdown periods, providing over 500,000 meals.

significant_events.source_quote: Prompt medical services for abandoned, injured or ill-treated homeless animals
significant_events.summary: Provided prompt medical services, shelter, and 24/7 care for abandoned, injured, or ill-treated homeless animals, rehabilitating 18,254 in 2020.

significant_events.source_quote: Vaccination and Sterilization of stray Dogs and Cats to reduce stray population and reduce Human- Animal transmittable diseases (Rabies etc.)
significant_events.summary: Conducts large-scale vaccination and sterilization drives for stray dogs and cats in Mumbai and Maharashtra to control population and rabies, vaccinating 8,000 and sterilizing 1,822 in 2020.

significant_events.source_quote: Adoption program for abandoned animals and orphaned puppies
significant_events.summary: Facilitates adoption for abandoned animals and orphaned puppies, successfully rehoming 2,489 animals in 2020 through a strict vetting process.

significant_events.source_quote: Behavioral rehabilitation of animals who have been mentally disturbed
significant_events.summary: Provides behavioral therapy for ill-treated, neglected, or abandoned animals, including retired police dogs, to help them recover and become fit for rehoming. Successfully rehabilitated 50 retired police dogs.

significant_events.source_quote: The organization started by conducting awareness programs in schools, slums, residential societies and various communities to educate individuals about what to do when they see a stray animal in distress.
significant_events.summary: Conducts awareness programs in schools, slums, residential societies, and communities to educate individuals on animal protection, welfare, and how to assist stray animals in distress.

data.original_text: Youth Organization in Defense of Animals (YODA), Mumbai was born with a vision to create awareness of the plight of stray and abandoned animals in India.
We mainly work with dogs and cats, we neuter and work on first aid cases, adoption, rescues, rehabilitation.
YODA is open 6 days a week for free of cost OPD for homeless animals.

YODA is known in the Mumbai region for providing a wide range of animal welfare activities like rescue, rehabilitation, rehoming, medical aid, background research for adopters, and medical emergencies when required.
The organization actively finds homes for stray dogs and pups and mass sterilizations stray animals in different zones across Mumbai.

The organization started by conducting awareness programs in schools, slums, residential societies and various communities to educate individuals about what to do when they see a stray animal in distress.
YODA's Mission is to develop a community that values and respects animals.
From educating youth to emergency services for stray animals, we now positively help and impact approx. 1000 animals each month across Maharashtra.

We encourage communities to choose adoption over purchase of animals, provide emergency mental and physical medical rehabilitation for animals in distress, educate urban, semi- urban and rural communities on the subject and work towards overall prevention of cruelty.

With your support, in 2020, we made immense progress in scaling up our sick and injured stray animal rehabilitation practice, rescued a large number of animals who were the victims of grave cruelty, and we scaled up our feeding program to cover over 5 Lakh meals for stray animals who were left starving on the road due to the pandemic.
YODA gets over 100 animals successfully adopted each month.
YODA Animal Welfare has been recognized at the Being Human Awards for its incredible work in rescue and rehabilitation.

name: Youth Organisation in Defence of Animals
